words.meaning: to leave something or stop doing it
words.example: The team will not abandon the project after one failure.

words.meaning: to officially end a law, system, or practice
words.example: The city may abolish the outdated parking rule.

words.meaning: to tell someone something confidently to remove doubt
words.example: The guide will assure visitors that the route is safe.

words.meaning: to describe the main quality of someone or something
words.example: High energy costs characterize the current economy.

words.meaning: to run after someone or something
words.example: Reporters chase the story after the mayor resigns.

words.meaning: to try to win against others
words.example: Small shops compete with online stores by improving service.

words.meaning: to make someone unable to understand clearly
words.example: Too many instructions can confuse new users.

words.meaning: to control or be much more important than others
words.example: One company can dominate a small market.

words.meaning: to try to achieve or follow something
words.example: She will pursue a career in public health.

words.meaning: to leave someone or something out
words.example: The survey should not exclude part-time workers.
